title: Importer: per-animation slicing (varying layouts + oversize/offset)
description: Extend the postprocessor from fixed 9x4 to per-animation grids. Each animation PNG has its own dimensions (walk 9x4 64px, hurt 6x1, shoot 13x4, oversize weapon/slash sheets at 128/192px with offsets that must realign to the 64x64 body cell). Read layout from sheet_definitions/animation metadata; slice + set pivots/offsets; emit per-animation frame sets feeding the runtime clip system.
close_reason: Per-animation slicing implemented: LpcSliceMath derives cell size from PNG vs clip rows/cols (oversize sheets slice on their own larger grid, no variant enumeration), postprocessor slices each animation by its grid and assembles ALL animations into LpcLayerSet.clips (walk also kept in legacy frames). 7 slice-math tests; full suite 30/30 EditMode + 25/25 offline; Editor+Runtime compile clean in Ultima4_2d. Exact oversize PIVOT offset (feet placement in big cells) deferred to follow-up (needs real art + sheet_definitions metadata).

title: Body-type system (male/female/child/skeleton + matching variants)
description: LPC bodies: male, muscular, female, pregnant, teen, child, skeleton, etc. EVERY clothing/part layer has a per-body-type variant (sheet_definitions layer_N.male/.female/.child...). A recipe must carry a body type; builder/importer resolves each layer matching variant; surface which parts support a chosen body type (disable mismatches in the creator).
close_reason: Body-type system: LpcBodyType (canonical types + per-type fallback chains + Resolve/Supports/Normalize, pure+tested). LpcLayerSet and LpcRecipe carry bodyType. Builder.ResolveLayers groups recipe variants by slot and picks the best body-type match per slot (fallback, drops unsupported slots, body-type swap re-resolves). Importer copies per-body-type <bodytype>/ subfolders (legacy fallback when source is already body-resolved), tags each IndexEntry+LpcLayerSet with bodyType. Tests: 8 shared (offline+Unity) + 6 EditMode build = +14; suite 44/44 EditMode, 33/33 offline; Editor+Runtime compile clean. Creator-UI disabling of mismatched parts consumes LpcBodyType.Supports/ResolveLayers and lands with the editor window (2g8.5). Importer folder resolution not run-verified here (no LPC art on disk).

title: Runtime: multi-animation clip system (all 17 LPC animations)
description: Replace the hardcoded walk in LpcCharacter/LpcAnimator with an animation-clip abstraction. The 17 LPC animations each have their OWN layout (walk 9x4; hurt 6x1; shoot 13x4; oversize variants) and timing. Need: clip defs (name -> rows/dirs/frameCount/frameSize/fps/loop-or-once), a play/queue API, per-animation frame arrays per layer (not one 36-array), animator selects clip by state. Walk first; architecture supports all 17.
design: Clip system: LpcClip struct (name/framesPerDir/directions/fps/loop/frameSize) + LpcClips static registry of all 17 ULPC anims in sheet order (walk-first values canonical; non-walk reconciled by 2g8.8). Per-layer frames stored per-animation via LpcClipFrames[] on LpcLayerSet; legacy flat 'frames' array kept and treated as the walk clip for backward compat with current importer. LpcCharacter is clip-aware: Play(clip) re-resolves+caches each layer's active frames, SetPose indexes dir*framesPerDir+frame for the active clip. LpcAnimator does locomotion (walk while moving / idle-or-stand) + PlayOnce(clip, queue) one-shot API with a queue that returns to locomotion. Behaviour identical to old walk-or-stand rig until idle/run/etc frames exist.
notes: Walk verified by construction (legacy frames -> walk clip path). idle/run/slash/etc become active automatically once 2g8.8 populates LpcLayerSet.clips. Frame counts for non-walk clips are standard ULPC values, tunable in LpcClips.
close_reason: Multi-animation clip system implemented (LpcClip/LpcClips/LpcClipMath/LpcClipFrames; clip-aware LpcCharacter + play/queue LpcAnimator). Walk verified via 18 shared NUnit tests passing offline. Non-walk clips light up once 2g8.8 populates per-animation frames.

title: Auto-slicing AssetPostprocessor -> LpcLayerSet
description: Auto-slicing AssetPostprocessor: any texture under Catalog/<slot>/ -> Sprite/Multiple sliced to that animation grid, PPU 32, feet pivot; OnPostprocessAllAssets generates/updates the LpcLayerSet (slot, zOrder, per-animation frames). Walk-first (9x4=36) but designed to extend to per-animation layouts - see the per-animation slicing issue.
close_reason: Auto-slicing AssetPostprocessor done. Catalog textures auto-config to Sprite/Multiple; grid derived from PNG dims (64px frame, animation-agnostic); PPU 32, Point, feet pivot (0.5,0); index = dir*cols+frame (top row first). OnPostprocessAllAssets generates LpcLayerSet assets from catalog_index.json (slot/zOrder/36 frames). Verified: 6 sheets -> 36 sprites each, 6 LayerSets correct, and a character built from them (CatalogStarter recipe) composites correctly (rendered PNG). Minor: postprocess re-runs per-texture within a batch (idempotent) - optimize later.

title: Migrate runtime scripts from Ultima4_2d into the package
description: Move LpcCharacter, LpcLayerSet, LpcRecipe, LpcCharacterBuilder, LpcCharacterSpawner (.cs + .meta, preserving GUIDs so existing .asset refs survive) into Runtime/ under namespace Lpc. Decouple the animator from PartyController (expose Facing/Walking fields the game sets). Update Ultima4 usings; verify compile + that StarterRecipe/LpcLayerSet assets still resolve.
close_reason: Migrated 6 runtime scripts into the package (namespace Lpc, GUIDs preserved); decoupled animator from PartyController via new ILpcMotion interface. Validated in Ultima4_2d: 0 compile errors, 9/9 SO assets intact, 0 missing scripts, play mode builds 6 layers + animator auto-wires via ILpcMotion.

title: Catalog manifest + selective copy script
description: Catalog source = the generator sheet_definitions/*.json (per-body-type paths, animation list, zPos/priority, credits/licenses) - the source of truth, NOT a hand-typed manifest. A selection (which categories/variants/body-type/animations to import) drives a selective copy of only those PNGs from the local LPC clone into the package Catalog/ layout. Volume control so Unity never chokes.
close_reason: Walk-first selective importer done: JSON manifest (selection) + LpcCatalogImporter (package Editor, menu Tools/LPC/Import Starter Catalog) copies only the listed walk.png into Catalog/<slot>/, writes catalog_index.json (slot/id/zOrder/source/animations/files) + a CREDITS stub. Verified: 6 parts copied at 576x256, index+credits written, 0 errors. Feeds 2g8.3 slicing. sheet_definitions-driven z-order=2g8.10, full credits=2g8.11.

title: LPC importer + runtime in the package
description: Move the layered LPC character runtime out of the Ultima4_2d game into this reusable package, and build the selective importer (manifest + copy + auto-slicing AssetPostprocessor), palette recolor, and an editor window that points at a local LPC clone. Art stays user-supplied (CC-BY-SA); importer emits attribution.
notes: TARGET = full LPC: all 17 animations (spellcast,thrust,walk,slash,shoot,hurt,watering,idle,jump,run,sit,emote,climb,combat,1h_slash,1h_backslash,1h_halfslash), 21 part categories, multiple body types. Implement walk-first; importer consumes sheet_definitions incl. credits. See new children for animation/body-type/coverage/attribution/samples scope.

title: Repo expert skill + refresh docs
description: Add .claude/skills/lpc-character-creator-expert (SKILL.md + references for architecture, workflow/conventions, art/licensing) as LLM-facing docs for this repo; fill in CLAUDE.md (build/test, architecture, conventions) and update README (clip system, body types, recolor, importer, art bundle, roadmap).
close_reason: Added .claude/skills/lpc-character-creator-expert (SKILL.md + architecture/workflow/art references) as versioned LLM docs for the repo; filled CLAUDE.md (build/test, architecture, conventions) and refreshed README (clip system, body types, recolor, importer, art bundle, roadmap).

title: Recolor across all animation clips (not just walk)
description: The palette recolor (LpcRecolor.RecolorFrames + LpcCharacter.SetLayerFrames) only recolors the active/walk clip, so recolored hair shows the new color on walk/idle but the ORIGINAL color on climb/jump/run/etc. - hair appears to change color per animation, and a color seam appears at the hairline. Fix: recolor every clip of a layer. Add LpcRecolor.RecolorClips(baseClips, ramp) + LpcCharacter.SetLayerClips(slot, clips, legacy); wire the consuming AppearanceSelector to recolor all clips from the LayerSet's base clips.
close_reason: Recolor now applies across all animation clips: added LpcRecolor.RecolorClips(baseClips, ramp) + LpcCharacter.SetLayerClips(slot, clips, legacy); AppearanceSelector (game) recolors every clip from the LayerSet base clips. Verified live - hair stays blonde through climb (was reverting to original orange) and the hairline seam is gone. +2 tests; 63/63 EditMode.

title: Animation selector menu + clip preview player (sample)
description: In the character-creation scene, add a vertical menu to the LEFT of the character that triggers each available animation. Needs: (1) LpcClipPlayer runtime component that plays a chosen clip looping (preview driver, advancing frames by clip fps); (2) a Samples asmdef (references Runtime + UnityEngine.UI) with LpcAnimationMenu that builds one button per clip the character actually has and plays it; (3) import the animations into the catalog and rebuild the MirrorCharacter so the clips exist. Depends on the LpcClips reconciliation.
close_reason: Delivered LpcClipPlayer (preview driver) + LpcAnimationMenu (Samples, self-populating button-per-clip). Demoed live in Ultima4_2d CharacterCreation scene: imported all 15 animations into the catalog (every LayerSet got 15 clips with correct frame counts), rebuilt MirrorCharacter, added a 15-button menu to the LEFT of the mirror, and triggered animations in play mode (run/spellcast animate correctly). 60/60 EditMode, 43/43 offline.

title: Reconcile LpcClips to the real ULPC animation set (15)
description: LpcClips was seeded from the epic notes (17, incl. watering/1h_*) but the actual ULPC universal body sheets are 15: spellcast(7x4) thrust(8x4) walk(9x4) slash(6x4) shoot(13x4) hurt(6x1) climb(6x1) idle(2x4) jump(5x4) run(8x4) sit(3x4) emote(3x4) combat_idle(2x4) backslash(13x4) halfslash(6x4). Rename combat->combat_idle, 1h_backslash->backslash, 1h_halfslash->halfslash (6 not 7), drop watering+1h_slash (not universal body sheets). Names must equal the on-disk file names so import==registry==playback. Update LpcClipsTests. Measured from D:/Projects/Universal-LPC-Spritesheet-Character-Generator.
close_reason: LpcClips reconciled to the 15 real ULPC universal animations with measured layouts; names now equal the on-disk PNG files (combat_idle/backslash/halfslash), dropped watering+1h_slash, halfslash fixed to 6 frames. Tests updated (15 + name-match assertions). 60/60 EditMode, 43/43 offline.

title: Per-direction z-order + auto sheet_definitions zPos parsing
description: 2g8.10 covers all 21 categories with definition-or-default z (per-entry zPos override) and behind-body negatives. Remaining: (1) per-direction z-order so a layer (e.g. cape) can render behind the body facing one way and in front facing another - needs LpcCharacter to re-apply sortingOrder per direction from an optional directional-z on the layer; (2) automatically read zPos (and multi-layer layer_1/layer_2) from the LPC sheet_definitions JSON during import instead of transcribing into the manifest; (3) enumerate available variants per category from the local clone (consumed by the editor window 2g8.5). Needs real LPC art to verify visually.

title: Oversize pivot/offset alignment (visual, real LPC art)
description: 2g8.8 slices oversize sheets on the correct derived cell (128/192px) and uses a bottom-center pivot, which is correct for standard 64px sheets and a reasonable default for oversize. Exact feet/body alignment inside an oversize cell varies by animation/body-type and needs the generator's offset metadata (sheet_definitions/custom_animation) plus visual verification against real imported LPC art. Implement per-animation oversize pivot offsets and verify a big-weapon swing lines up with the 64px body.

title: Animation pathway integration tests (build->animate->sprite)
description: Unit tests cover pure clip math but not the runtime pathway. Add Unity-only EditMode integration tests that build an LpcCharacter (real GameObject + Sprites), attach LpcAnimator, drive it deterministically via Tick(dt), and assert the SpriteRenderer advances: initial standing pose, walk cycle stays in the correct direction row and skips frame 0, facing changes pick the right row, stopping returns to standing, and PlayOnce one-shot plays through then returns to locomotion. Refactored LpcAnimator: extract Update body into public Tick(float dt) + lazy EnsureInit so it is deterministically testable in EditMode.
close_reason: Added 5 EditMode integration tests (Tests/Integration/LpcAnimationPathwayTests) exercising the built character pathway with real GameObjects/Sprites; refactored LpcAnimator to public Tick(dt)+EnsureInit for deterministic testing. Ran in Ultima4_2d Test Runner: 23/23 EditMode pass (18 logic + 5 pathway). Offline dotnet bridge still 18/18 (Integration subfolder excluded by non-recursive glob).

title: Attribution/credits generation from sheet_definitions
description: Each sheet_definition JSON carries per-part credits: authors, licenses (CC-BY-SA / GPL / OGA-BY), notes, source URLs. The importer must aggregate these for every part ACTUALLY used and emit an attribution/CREDITS file (LPC licenses require artist credit). Wire into the copy/import step so attribution stays current with the selected layers.
close_reason: Attribution generation: LpcCredits (Runtime, pure+tested) aggregates unique authors/licenses across used parts (case-insensitive dedupe, order-preserving) and formats a compliant CREDITS doc - reusable for an in-game credits screen. Editor LpcCreditsReader parses the LPC clone's CREDITS.csv (canonical ULPC artifact): header-based column detection, quote-aware RFC4180 parsing, matches rows under each part source, merges manifest credit overrides, degrades to a 'credits not found' note. Importer now emits CREDITS.txt via LpcCredits.Format with one record per part actually copied (deduped by source). Tests: 5 shared (offline+Unity) LpcCredits + 3 Editor LpcCreditsReader (synthetic CSV); suite 56/56 EditMode, 42/42 offline; clean compile. Automatic sheet_definitions-embedded credit parsing (if a clone uses that instead of CREDITS.csv) folded into 2g8.15.

title: Full part coverage (21 categories) + z-order from definitions
description: Expand beyond the 6 starter slots to all 21 LPC categories (arms, backpack, beards, body, cape, dress, eyes, facial, feet, hair, hat, head, legs, neck, quiver, shadow, shield, shoulders, tools, torso, weapon). Drive z-order from each definition zPos/priority incl. multi-layer parts (layer_1/layer_2) and behind-body layers (cape back, quiver, per-direction). Enumerate available variants per category from the local clone.
close_reason: Full 21-category coverage + default z model: LpcCategory (canonical 21 categories, back-to-front DefaultZ incl. behind-body negatives shadow/backpack/cape/quiver, IsKnown/IsBehindBody; pure+tested). Importer replaces the 14-slot table with LpcCategory.DefaultZ and adds per-entry zPos override (transcribe a definition's value); multi-layer parts = multiple entries on one slot (runtime already orders any z, so multi-layer/behind-body work with no runtime change). Tests: +4 shared LpcCategory; suite 48/48 EditMode, 37/37 offline; Editor+Runtime compile clean. Deferred to 2g8.15: per-direction z (cape flip), automatic sheet_definitions zPos parsing, variant enumeration for the creator UI.

title: Palette recolor (runtime palette-swap)
description: Read LPC palette_definitions ramps; luminance-order the base 6-shade ramp and remap to a target color ramp at runtime, so style (layer) x color (recolor) is N+M not NxM. Already prototyped for hair in the game; generalize here.
close_reason: Runtime palette-swap recolor done. LpcPalette (ScriptableObject; 20 hair ramps baked from palette_definitions via LpcPaletteImporter, menu Tools/LPC/Import Hair Palette). LpcRecolor: detects base shades, remaps shade-for-shade onto a target ramp (luminance-ordered, alpha preserved), recolors the shared sheet ONCE + re-slices, cached. LpcCharacter.SetLayerFrames swaps frames live. Catalog textures marked readable. Verified: hair recolored base/black/blue/blonde, shading preserved, only hair changed (rendered). Manifest padded to 4 hairs + 3 shirts for cycling. Skin/cloth recolor reuse the same path later.

title: Editor window: point at LPC repo, browse + pick layers
description: A window with the LPC clone path setting, a browser of available slots/options, and checkboxes that write into catalog_manifest.json + run the copy. WoW-style preview optional.
close_reason: Catalog builder EditorWindow (Tools/LPC/Catalog Window): set LPC source path, pick body types + animations, browse each of the 21 categories (lazy per-category scan), check parts, Write Manifest & Import (writes catalog_manifest.json + runs LpcCatalogImporter). Pure part-grouping in LpcSourceLayout (offline-tested, 4 tests) + LpcSourceScanner (filesystem). Verified against the real LPC source (feet=508 parts x15 anims, hair=169). 69 EditMode + 47 offline pass. Window UI not screenshot-verifiable via the bridge; logic verified. WoW-style preview not included (optional).

title: Handle parts missing an animation (formal shirt vanish)
description: A worn part may lack a sheet for an animation (formal shirt has only 6 of 15: no climb/jump/run/etc.). It currently hides (bare torso). Decide+implement policy: (a) menu only offers animations all worn parts support, (b) fall back to a neutral frame, or (c) keep hide. Likely (a) for the creator UI.
close_reason: Chose transparency over restriction: keep all animations available but surface coverage. Runtime: LpcLayerSet.SupportedClips/MissingClips + LpcCharacter.SlotsMissingClip. UI: LpcAnimationMenu flags incomplete clips (amber + *) and a status line reports which worn parts hide for a clicked clip ('jump: hidden (no jump art): torso'); AppearanceSelector label shows '(6/15 anims)'. Verified live with the formal shirt. +2 tests, 65/65 EditMode.

title: Bundle full LPC art set with per-artist attribution (drop-in)
description: Intent: bundle ALL LPC character art + animations in the package for a drop-in solution, under their licenses (CC-BY-SA/GPL/OGA-BY) with complete per-artist attribution (generated via LpcCreditsReader/LpcCredits). Dual-license: code MIT, art CC-BY-SA/etc. with CREDITS.md + LICENSE-ART.txt. Do this AFTER the system is fully working. Start with a starter set in Samples~, then scale to the full spritesheets tree.
close_reason: Full art bundle shipped as GitHub Release art-v1: LpcArt-full.zip (212MB, 144,699 PNGs + CREDITS.csv + CREDITS.md + LICENSE-ART.txt) built by tools/Build_Art_Release.ps1. Consumer Editor/LpcArtImporter (Tools/LPC/Import Bundled Art) downloads + extracts to LpcArtSource/ outside Assets and repoints the manifest; selective importer slices chosen parts. Code MIT, art CC-BY-SA/GPL/OGA-BY w/ per-file CREDITS.csv. CAVEAT: download 404s while repo is PRIVATE - works once public.

title: Samples: demo character + mini character-creation scene
description: Package Samples~/: a demo recipe + scene with an animated multi-layer character, and a minimal character-creation scene (slot cycling + recolor) so the package is usable/testable standalone, independent of Ultima4_2d.

title: OpenGameArt scraping for missing LPC assets
description: Later: scrape opengameart.org for LPC-compatible layers not present in the base generator clone. Respect licenses + attribution.

value: Unity test bed = Ultima4_2d at C:/UnityProjects/Ultima4_2d (Unity 6000.4.3f1), references this package via manifest file:D:/Projects/LPC-Unity-Character-Creator with testables added (EditMode tests show in Test Runner). MCP for Unity server registered USER scope http://127.0.0.1:8080/mcp; native tools (run_tests/manage_editor/execute_code/manage_camera) load after a Claude Code restart. LPC art source clone: D:/Projects/Universal-LPC-Spritesheet-Character-Generator (per-animation PNGs: 15 universal anims). Catalog + manifest at Ultima4_2d/Assets/Characters/LPC (manifest now imports all 15 animations). Scene Assets/Scenes/CharacterCreation.unity has MirrorCharacter (LpcCharacter) shown via MirrorCamera + CreationCanvas UI. To run EditMode tests: run_tests assembly TCLowe.Lpc.EditMode.Tests.
